tluaGenericForErrors.tlua(3,12): error TLUA2349: This expression is not callable.
tluaGenericForErrors.tlua(22,7): error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.
tluaGenericForErrors.tlua(29,9): error TLUA1005: 'in' expected.
tluaGenericForErrors.tlua(29,9): error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'of'.
tluaGenericForErrors.tlua(36,20): error TLUA2349: This expression is not callable.
tluaGenericForErrors.tlua(36,20): error TLUA2448: Block-scoped variable 'k' used before its declaration.
tluaGenericForErrors.tlua(36,20): error TLUA2454: Variable 'k' is used before being assigned.


==== tluaGenericForErrors.tlua (7 errors) ====
    // Value 1 must be callable — reported once.
    function notCallable(): void
      for k in 42 do
               ~~
!!! error TLUA2349: This expression is not callable.
        k;
      end
    end
    
    // An `any` iterator types the names as any, silently.
    declare anything: any;
    
    function anyIterator(): void
      for k, v in anything do
        k;
        v;
      end
    end
    
    // An annotation that rejects the positional value.
    declare function iter(t: string[], i: number): (number, string?);
    
    function badAnnotation(t: string[]): void
      for k: boolean in iter do
          ~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.
        k;
      end
    end
    
    // Missing `in`.
    function missingIn(): void
      for k of do
            ~~
!!! error TLUA1005: 'in' expected.
            ~~
!!! error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'of'.
      end
    end
    
    // Loop names are ordinary block-scoped variables: using one in its own
    // header is a use-before-declaration error (like numeric-for).
    function selfReference(): void
      for k: string in k do
                       ~
!!! error TLUA2349: This expression is not callable.
                       ~
!!! error TLUA2448: Block-scoped variable 'k' used before its declaration.
!!! related TLUA2728 tluaGenericForErrors.tlua:36:7: 'k' is declared here.
                       ~
!!! error TLUA2454: Variable 'k' is used before being assigned.
        k;
      end
    end
